name: hud_ui_composite_panel_constructor_with_entry_count
description: HudUiCompositePanel::ConstructorWithEntryCount constructs the panel base, initializes the entry vector, installs the composite ftable, builds default transition-text entries, clears and hides entries through the resize/reapply path, lays out at the origin, marks the panel visible, and returns self.
known_limits: Depends on the composite-panel vector/entry helpers and HudUiPanel formatting/layout helpers, which are functionally accepted but remain binary-safe debt.

Binary Ninja still represents the original MSVC constructor EH state, empty allocator byte storage, and inlined vector resize shape as low-level artifacts; byte-safe shape should be revisited with the full composite-panel class/source-file pass.

VC5SP3 cl 11.00.7022 x86 /O2 /Ob1 /GX /Zp4 /FAcs COFF byte comparison for ?ConstructorWithEntryCount@HudUiCompositePanel@@QAEPAU1@H@Z at 0x4bb790 still fails: 417 unmasked mismatches, 40 relocation bytes masked, 14 trailing VC NOP bytes trimmed, Binary Ninja body is 461 bytes and the VC object symbol is 176 bytes.
binary_safe_evidence: Binary Ninja shows HudUiPanel::ConstructorDefault, vector begin/end/capacity zeroing, composite ftable install, template transition-text entry construction/destruction, entry vector resize to entryCount, empty text setup, layout, resize/reapply, virtual SetVisible(true), and return of self.

The native constructor smoke validates return identity, composite ftable installation, entry-vector count/capacity, transition-text entry ftable installation, cleared/hidden entries, origin layout, and final visible panel state.
